data.title: Privia Health partners with Integrated Medical Services to expand in Arizona
data.description:
• Privia Health Group (NASDAQ:PRVA) said it has partnered with Integrated Medical Services to enter Arizona, with about 70 physicians and advanced practice providers in 21 locations. 
  • "With this transaction, Privia Health launches Privia Medical Group—Arizona," the company said.
  • Integrated Medical Services will be implemented on the Privia Platform in the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • The company  expects the launch in Arizona to be profitable starting in the fourth quarter of 2025 and for full-year 2026.

data.title: Argentine lawmakers launch investigation into Libra crypto scandal
data.description:
Argentina's lower house of Congress opted to launch a probe into the Libra cryptocurrency, which President Javier Milei hyped before its sudden collapse cost investors hundreds of millions of dollars, according to a recent release.

The country's Chamber of Deputies approved three draft resolutions regarding Libra, including the establishment of an investigative commission, the subpoena of top government officials and an inquiry made to the national government.

Chief of Cabinet Guillermo Francos, Economy Minister Luis Caputo, Justice Minister Mariano Cúneo Libarona and the National Securities Commission chief Roberto Silva were among those summoned, the Aug. 8 release published by the Chamber of Deputies in Argentina noted.

The measures sailed through with solid support, though strong pushback exposed deep partisan divides. 

Milei's brief social media endorsement of the Libra earlier in the year turned sour as an estimated 86% of traders that took stake in the memecoin lost a combined $251M in a swift crash, while "a group of insiders" managed to rake in $180M, research firm Nansen had noted.

data.title: Samsung partners with Google to bring Gemini to its AI robot companion
data.description:
Samsung's (SNLLF) new artificial intelligence-powered home robot companion, Ballie, will utilize Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) Gemini, its popular large language model.

Ballie, which is slated for release this summer, will engage with users through "natural" conversation to help manage households, tackling tasks such as greeting visitors, personalizing schedules, lighting, and even projecting videos.

A short video by Samsung shows the yellow, volleyball-sized Ballie wheeling around a household giving a mother hairstyling advice, showing a kid the latest trending shorts and informing dad he is late for work. 

"Through this partnership, Samsung and Google Cloud are redefining the role of AI in the home," said Yongjae Kim, Executive Vice President of the Visual Display Business at Samsung Electronics. "By pairing Gemini's powerful multimodal reasoning with Samsung’s AI capabilities in Ballie, we're leveraging the power of open collaboration to unlock a new era of personalized AI companion — one that moves with users, anticipates their needs and interacts in more dynamic and meaningful ways than ever before."

The new partnership for Ballie is a natural extension for these two companies. Last year, Samsung's Galaxy S24 smartphone series integrated Gemini through Google Cloud.

"With Gemini on Google Cloud, Samsung is demonstrating how to deploy generative AI at scale, integrating it directly into the heart of their popular products," said Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian.

data.title: Eastman Chemical upgraded at RBC Capital despite tariff hurdles
data.description:
Eastman Chemical (NYSE:EMN) on Wednesday was upgraded to Outperform from a previous investment rating of Sector Perform by analysts at RBC Capital Markets. They said the recent post-tariff announcement selloff presented a compelling buying opportunity.

While Eastman (NYSE:EMN) may face challenges from new tariffs and potential weakness in durable goods, the company is comparatively better positioned than peers due to its limited exposure to export-heavy plastics markets, Arun Viswanathan, research analyst at RBC, said in an April 9 report.

“Eastman (EMN) could see headwinds from tariffs/retaliation and potentially weaker durables, but likely less than peers as Eastman (EMN) is not a major plastics exporter,” he said.

The firm expects low-single-digit ebitda growth from AFP [additives and functional products] improvement and $50 million in Kingsport contributions, allowing Eastman (EMN) to meet its $8.00 to $8.75 fiscal 2025 guidance — even if at the lower end.

RBC revised its first-quarter and full-year 2025 ebitda estimates down slightly, from $436 million and $1.91 billion to $430 million and $1.85 billion, respectively.

The firm also lowered its price target for Eastman (EMN) shares to $91 from $103, based on estimated enterprise value that’s 8 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and and amortization, down from multiple of 8.5 times, to account for potential tariff and natural gas cost pressures.

Despite the lowered estimates, Viswanathan sees significant upside.

“Even after our downward revisions, we believe Eastman (EMN) is trading below a reasonable valuation, with over 20% implied upside to our price target,” he said.

Lower tariff exposure than peers

RBC pointed to Eastman's (EMN) lower international exposure as a key differentiator. About 40% of the company’s revenue comes from U.S. sales, and its exposure to Mexico, Canada and China remains minimal — low single-digit percentages for Mexico and Canada, and approximately 10% for China. This contrasts with other chemical firms that rely more heavily on exports, particularly in segments like PVC and polyethylene.

“Given chemical chains export anywhere from about 20% (e.g. caustic) to 40% (polyvinyl chloride) or higher (polyethylene), we see Eastman’s (EMN) overall exposure of low double digits as a relative advantage to peers,” Viswanathan said.

End-market stability, upside from Kingsport

RBC also highlighted Eastman's (EMN) expectations for low single-digit growth in stable end markets, which account for about 45% of company revenues. While sectors like transportation (18% of revenue) and durables (10%) may see continued softness, building and construction (11%) could benefit from lower interest rates and broader economic shifts driven by tariffs.

“We expect low-single-digit organic growth for Eastman (EMN) in 2025,” Viswanathan said, noting that $50 million in contributions from the Kingsport methanolysis plant and modest tailwinds from lower interest expenses and share buybacks (approximately $0.25 a share) could help support earnings.

RBC now forecasts fiscal 2025 EPS of between $8.00 and $8.35, compared with its previous range of $8.75 and consensus estimates of $8.44. Fiscal 2026 EPS is now estimated at $9.20, down from $9.78 previously.

Despite the adjustments, RBC sees Eastman (EMN) as undervalued and well-positioned relative to peers.

“We see the recent selloff as a buying opportunity,” Viswanathan said.

RBC on the same day downgraded LyondellBasell (LYB).

data.title: Tenet Healthcare is Best Idea as Guggenheim launches coverage of 11 healthcare names
data.description:
Tenet Healthcare (NYSE:THC) traded higher in the early hours on Tuesday as Guggenheim launched the coverage of 11 healthcare names across managed care, behavioral health, and care delivery, naming the hospital operator its Best Idea.

Guggenheim analyst Jason Cassorla issued a Buy rating and a $165 per share target on Tenet (NYSE:THC), arguing that the company deserved a multiple re-rating due to improvements in its capital structure and free cash flow generation.

The analyst also issued Buy ratings on healthcare providers Acadia Healthcare (NASDAQ:ACHC), Ardent Health Partners (ARDT), and Universal Health Services (UHS), with their price targets set to $36, $16, and $208 per share, respectively.

Unlike managed care organizations, Cassorla said that the hospital sector has benefited from fundamental developments that occurred during the pandemic. “Reflecting on these considerations, we’re pulled toward the notion of mean reversion for the group, with volume, revenue/mix, and cost developments reverting to historical growth patterns,” Cassorla added.

The analyst was positive on the long-term outlook of the behavioral health industry in particular due to a growing need for mental healthcare, the rising cases of addiction, and other sector-specific factors.

Meanwhile, HCA Healthcare (HCA), the largest for-profit hospital chain in the U.S., and Community Health Systems (CYH) received Guggenheim's Neutral ratings. As for HCA, the analyst opted to stay on the sideline due to factors such as Medicaid reimbursement headwinds and a valuation, which he said stands in line with the stock's historical averages.

In managed care, Guggenheim launched Cigna (NYSE:CI), Humana (HUM), and Elevance Health (ELV) with Buy recommendations noting a neutral to favorable outlook for the payor group with their price targets set to $384, $326, and $518 per share, respectively. Meanwhile, Centene (CNC) and Molina Healthcare (MOH) received Neutra ratings mainly due to policy uncertainty surrounding the Medicaid market.

data.title: First quarter PC shipments surge ahead of tariff announcements
data.description:
• Global PC shipments (desktops, notebooks and workstations) grew 9.4% to 62.7M units in Q1 2025, driven by OEMs accelerating shipments to the US ahead of tariff changes.
  • Lenovo led the market with 15.2M units shipped in Q1, growing 10.7% year-on-year.
  • Apple shipments grew by 22% with 6.5M units in Q1.
  • HP shipped 12.8M units in Q1, a 6.1% increase from last year.
  • Dell saw 3% growth Y/Y, shipping 9.5M units in Q1.
  • Asus shipped 4M units in Q1, up 8.8% Y/Y.
  • Major PC manufacturers are diversifying production to countries like Vietnam, Thailand, and India to mitigate the impact of tariffs, with plans to reduce US-bound shipments from China by 2025.
  • Tariffs on consumer demand are expected to increase PC prices, particularly affecting SMBs and potentially slowing momentum for the critical transition away from Windows 10.
  • Source: Data from Canalys

data.title: CME Group reports 13% Y/Y rise in Q1 average daily volume
data.description:
• Derivatives marketplace CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) announced on Wednesday a record Q1 average daily volume of 29.8M contracts, an increase of 13% from the same period last year.
  • International ADV stood at 8.8M contracts, up 19% year-over-year.
  • "This quarterly record was driven by growth across all asset classes, as well as record quarterly ADV in EMEA, APAC and Canada," CME said in a statement.
  • EMEA ADV rose 20% from last year to 6.5M contracts, driven by a 32% increase in Agricultural products ADV.
  • APAC ADV hit 2M contracts, up 20% from the prior year. The growth was driven by a 31% rise in Energy products ADV, 27% in Equity Index, and 21% in Agricultural products.
  • Canada ADV reached 196,000 contracts in Q1, an increase of 17% on a yearly basis. Meanwhile, LatAm ADV fell 2% to 185,000 contracts.
  • "Amid continued economic and geopolitical uncertainty in Q1, clients turned to CME Group markets in record numbers to manage their risk and unlock trading opportunities," said Senior Managing Director and Chief Commercial Officer Julie Winkler.

data.title: Caterpillar declares $1.41 dividend
data.description:
• Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) declares $1.41/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.07%
  • Payable May 20; for shareholders of record April 21; ex-div April 21.
  • See CAT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Dayforce announces Tampa Bay Buccaneers expanded use of Dayforce platform
data.description:
• Dayforce (NYSE:DAY) has announced the Tampa Bay Buccaneers have expanded their use of the Dayforce™ platform to further enhance the employee experience, modernize HR processes, and navigate the unique compliance challenges of professional sports.
  • As part of this expansion, the Buccaneers will integrate Dayforce Performance Management and Compensation Management into their existing Dayforce suite, reinforcing their commitment to leveraging best-in-class technology to support, develop, and manage their growing team.
  • The Dayforce AI-powered platform has supercharged the Buccaneers’ employee experience by empowering its 850+ full-time and seasonal employees with HR self-service tools, all available on the Dayforce mobile app.
  • This digital transformation has helped eliminate manual processes, enabled a self-service approach to workforce management, and enhanced the overall employee experience.

data.title: Crude inventory rose by 2.6M barrels for week ended April 4 – EIA
data.description:
Commercial crude stocks for the week ended April 4: 442.3M barrels.

Crude inventory change: 2.6M barrels vs. 6.2M barrels for the week ended March 28.

Consensus estimate 2.200M.

Gasoline inventory change: -1.6M barrels vs. -1.6M barrels for the week ended March 28. 

Consensus estimate -1.700M.

Distillates inventory change: -3.5M barrels vs. 0.3M barrels for the week ended March 28. 

Consensus estimate 0.200M.

SPR:  0.3M vs 0.3M for the week ended March 28.

Futures (CL1:COM)  down 3.8% $57.31bbl.

 

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:DBO), (USL).
